what_it_does: Acceptance test suite for the wire's build-close checkpoint: verifies that every verified, in-scope autonomous build is committed as a single revertible [self-build] git commit before being marked implemented, while failed, out-of-scope, empty, or uncommitable builds never commit and are re-surfaced.
observations: This file is a self-runnable Python test (run .venv/bin/python tests/wire_commit_acceptance.py) with a global PASS counter and check/raises helpers.
It injects stub launchers, verifiers, and a RecordingCommitter so no real subprocess or git commit touches the live repo; stores are fresh temp FsStore instances.
It tests five scenarios: successful commit with exact changed_delta, concurrent-writer isolation, verification failure, out-of-scope paths, commit failure, and empty/plan-mode deltas.
It also exercises the real default committer _self_build_commit against a throwaway git repo to prove the [self-build] prefix, path-scoped add, and revertibility.
It records and asserts the checkpoint sha on the build item, the decision.implemented event, the surfaced review item, and the dispatch return.
standouts: Safety framing: described as 'the wire's GIT CHECKPOINT (Tim's safety mandate before arming)'.
RecordingCommitter stub captures exactly which paths and message are passed, enabling isolation assertions without touching git.
Commit-failure path is deliberately surfaced via a decision.verify terminal event so resurface_crashed does not treat it as a crashed dispatch.
Default committer test uses a real but temporary git repository to verify [self-build] subject prefix and that concurrent dirty files remain unstaged.
conventions: Module-level docstring states purpose, safety claim, coverage, and run command.
Helper factories (fresh_suite, approve_seq, good_launch) reduce boilerplate.
Stub classes implement callables matching production seams (launcher, verifier, committer).
Uses next(...) without default to fetch expected events; assumes event ordering.
concerns: Tests call private/underscore methods and attributes such as s._self_build_commit, s.dispatch_decision, s.is_build_intent, and impl.resurface_crashed, coupling tests to internal implementation.
Assumes specific event kinds (decision.implemented, decision.verify, decision.dispatch) and review payload fields not defined in this file.
The default committer test still shells out to real git; although against a temp repo, it depends on git being installed and available.
Temp directories and git repos are created but not explicitly cleaned up.
The global PASS counter and check() mutation make tests order-dependent and not thread-safe.
notes: This is the safety-gate acceptance test for autonomous build commits: it proves the wire only closes builds that are verified, in-scope, and successfully checkpointed as a single revertible [self-build] commit.
questions: What is the exact contract of dispatch_decision's return shape (closed, status, commit, requeued, overrun, review_surfaced)?
How does the default critic distinguish a plan-mode empty delta from a legitimate empty build when a verifier is injected?
What happens if the committer returns a sha but the subsequent git state no longer matches the recorded delta?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is an acceptance test for git checkpoint behavior before marking builds implemented.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud retry rather than silent skip
Model-free, repo-safe testing via injected seams
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce that autonomous builds are never irreversibly applied: every accepted change must first become a single, path-scoped, [self-build] git commit whose sha is recorded, so the whole build can be undone with one git revert; anything that cannot meet this bar is surfaced again rather than closed.
novelty: It treats git commit failure as a first-class terminal verification outcome (decision.verify) rather than a crash, and provides a fake-repo acceptance test for the default [self-build] committer's prefix and isolation behavior.
contribution: Provides acceptance criteria and injectable seam specifications for the wire's build-close checkpoint, plus a repo-safe verification that the default committer produces a path-scoped, revertible [self-build] commit.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the wire's autonomous-build git checkpoint, verifying that verified in-scope builds are committed as a single [self-build] revertible commit and that failed, out-of-scope, empty, or uncommitable builds are re-surfaced without committing.
intention_for_embedding: To guarantee that every accepted autonomous build is one git revert away from being undone and that no build is marked implemented without a successful checkpoint.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: The check(label, cond) function asserts the given condition, increments the module-level PASS counter on success, and prints a formatted ok line; on failure it raises an AssertionError with the label.

symbols.does: Helper that asserts a callable raises a specific exception type.
symbols.description: The raises(label, fn, exc=GovernanceError) function runs fn, expects it to raise exc, and calls check on success; if a different or no exception is raised, it fails with a descriptive AssertionError.

symbols.does: Factory that creates a fresh Suite with a temporary FsStore and discovered nodes.
symbols.description: The fresh_suite() function builds a new temporary directory, instantiates FsStore inside it, discovers nodes from the NODES directory, and returns a Suite instance so each test starts isolated.

symbols.does: Approves a surfaced build intent and returns the resulting event sequence number.
symbols.description: The approve_seq(suite, sid) function resolves the surfaced item with approve, locates the matching resolve event in the store, and returns its seq value for use in dispatch_decision.

symbols.does: Factory returning a stub launcher that reports success with a given changed_files list.
symbols.description: The good_launch(changed) function returns a launcher callable that simulates a finished, successful build in acceptEdits permission mode, reporting the supplied files as changed_files.

symbols.does: Stub git committer that records staged paths and message and returns a configurable fake sha.
symbols.description: The RecordingCommitter class implements the committer seam used by dispatch_decision; it records every (paths, msg) call and returns either a deterministic fake sha or None to simulate commit failure, enabling tests to assert exact staging behavior without touching git.

symbols.does: Initializes the stub committer with a configurable fake sha and an empty call log.
symbols.description: The __init__(self, sha='a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0') method stores the configured sha in self._sha, initializes self.calls as an empty list, and sets up the instance to act as a callable committer stub.

symbols.does: Records a commit attempt and returns the configured fake sha or None.
symbols.description: The __call__(self, paths, msg) method appends a dict containing the staged paths and message to self.calls, then returns self._sha, which is either a fake 40-character sha for success or None to simulate a git commit failure.
